title: Grit's AI agents pass 99% of Git's tests in Rust rewrite
subtitle: Grit, a project from GitButler, is rewriting Git in Rust using AI coding agents. The effort targets a foundational developer tool and tests how far agent-driven development can scale on a large, complex codebase.

title: CEOs who see AI as staff replacement are failing
subtitle: TechDirt argues that CEOs who believe AI can simply replace their workforce are poor leaders, conflating automation potential with workforce strategy. The piece pushes back against the Silicon Valley narrative that AI displacement is inevitable or even desirable.

title: Saylor buys as Bitcoin slips below $60,000
subtitle: Bitcoin steadied after slipping below $60,000, with Strategy Chairman Michael Saylor resuming his buying streak. Saylor's continued accumulation during the dip signals persistent institutional conviction despite the recent price weakness.

title: Robots Are Critical to US Manufacturing, Says Standard Bots CEO
subtitle: Standard Bots raised $200 million to scale US manufacturing of robotic arms, CEO Evan Beard said on Bloomberg Tech. The funding comes as the US races to close the gap with China on advanced robotics production.

title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO as Traders Bet on $1.5T Valuation
subtitle: OpenAI confidentially filed its S-1 for a public listing later this year, joining AI rivals heading to public markets. The move places it in a $3.6 trillion AI IPO pipeline, though PitchBook warns it may be the costliest bet.

title: Anthropic ships Fable 5, the public face of Mythos
subtitle: Anthropic released a Mythos-like AI model to the public, two months after a private preview reportedly moved markets. The public launch gives developers and enterprises access to capabilities that had previously been limited to invited testers.

title: Claude Mythos turns N-day patches into exploit windows
subtitle: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 to trusted cyber partners and Claude Fable 5, a restricted public version, to everyone else. The split release shows the lab balancing capable AI with misuse risks.

title: Anthropic gives you Fable 5. The good version is for the government.
subtitle: Anthropic is widely releasing a version of its Mythos AI model with cybersecurity features disabled. The release comes months after the company warned the model could find and exploit vulnerabilities in critical software.

title: Google Cloud retires the AI pilot for government
subtitle: Google Cloud launched Gemini for Government, an AI deployment blueprint for public sector agencies moving beyond isolated pilots. It targets federal, state, and local organizations seeking productivity gains in mission delivery.

title: Transload pivots from forklift AI to camera-based dimensioning
subtitle: Julius, Jago, and Nils launched Transload through YC P26, building software that measures LTL freight dimensions using security cameras already installed in trucking terminals. The approach replaces dedicated dimensioning stations, potentially cutting hardware costs for carriers.

title: Sabertooth VC routes $400M to AI startups without a fund
subtitle: Justin Ernest, founder of Sabertooth VC, deployed nearly $400 million into Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX via a captive LP network, skipping the year-long fundraise cycle. The structure lets him move capital into competitive deals faster than traditional VCs.

title: ASML hits record high, trades at years-low valuation
subtitle: ASML reached fresh record highs in European markets, yet the stock remains at its cheapest relative valuation versus peers in years. The gap between surging price and lagging multiples signals possible upside if it closes.
